verdicts.note: Quote "Modalities: mri" appears verbatim in e2 and directly establishes modality_primary = mri.

Quote matches e2 verbatim ("The release includes T1w anatomical scans, resting-state fMRI, and task fMRI runs related to scientific creativity and verbal creativity.") and supports the three listed data types.
Value is null with an evidence-gap rationale rather than an asserted claim; the source text indeed never gives a health/diagnostic status, only task names, so the null + needs statement is accurate.
Quote "DatasetDOI: doi:10.18112/openneuro.ds008055.v1.0.4" appears verbatim in e2 and establishes the DOI.
Quote "License: CC0" appears verbatim in e2 and directly establishes the license.
Quote "OpenNeuro dataset ds008055 (snapshot 1.0.4)" appears verbatim in e2 and establishes provider = OpenNeuro.
Value is null with an evidence-gap rationale; e2 indeed contains no country/city/institution field, so the null + needs statement is accurate.
Quote "Number of subjects: 108" appears verbatim in e2 and directly establishes n_subjects.
Value is null with an evidence-gap rationale; e2 only contains an upload timestamp (Created: 2026-06-28T15:33:11.402Z), not an acquisition date range, so the null + needs statement is accurate.
Value is null with an evidence-gap rationale; e2 never states "human" verbatim (it is only implied by MRI/participant language), so the null + needs statement is accurate.
Value is null with an evidence-gap rationale; e2 states a license (CC0) but never an explicit cost/terms-of-use statement, so the null + needs statement is accurate.

Both quotes ("License: CC0" and "This OpenNeuro copy is a public, defaced release derived from the project BIDS directory.") appear verbatim in e2 and together reasonably support access = open (CC0 public-domain license + explicit description as a public release).
Quote "This OpenNeuro copy is a public, defaced release derived from the project BIDS directory." appears verbatim in e2 and matches the value exactly.

The cited task-SCT quote only describes one of four tasks in the release (rest, SCT, ID, TVC) and the dataset's own data_types field lists T1w anatomical scans and resting-state fMRI alongside task fMRI. Characterizing the whole dataset as simply a 'Task fMRI dataset on scientific creativity' overgeneralizes from a single-task quote and omits the anatomical/resting-state modalities and the 'verbal creativity' scope explicitly present in the same source (e2: 'related to scientific creativity and verbal creativity'). The quotes sit near the claim but do not establish the summary's overall framing.

Same issue as short_desc_en (this is its Spanish translation, citing identical evidence): the task-SCT quote covers only one of four tasks and ignores the anatomical/resting-state data and 'verbal creativity' scope documented elsewhere in e2, so labeling the dataset as solely a task-fMRI creativity dataset is not established by the cited quotes.
